# Instant Orthopedic Care

> Walk-in orthopedic urgent care clinic in Draper, Utah offering same-day treatment for fractures, sprains, sports injuries, and chronic joint pain by board-certified orthopedic specialists with on-site X-ray—no appointment or referral needed.

Instant Orthopedic Care (IOC) is a walk-in orthopedic urgent care clinic located at 75 E 12300 S Suite 100, Draper, UT 84020. We serve patients throughout Salt Lake County and Utah County, including Sandy, South Jordan, Riverton, Herriman, Lehi, Alpine, Highland, American Fork, Pleasant Grove, Bluffdale, and Midvale.

- Phone: (385) 386-7026
- Email: info@instantorthopediccare.com
- Hours: Mon–Fri 9:00 AM – 6:30 PM, Sat 9:00 AM – 2:00 PM, Sun 9:00 AM – 12:00 PM
- Walk-ins welcome. No appointment or referral needed.
- Most major insurance accepted including BCBS, Aetna, Cigna, UnitedHealthcare, Medicaid, Medicare, and workers' compensation.
- Average wait time: under 15 minutes.
- On-site digital X-ray for immediate diagnosis.
- Cost: up to 75% less than the emergency room.

## Main Pages

- [Home](https://instantorthopediccare.com/): Overview of the clinic, comparison to ER and urgent care, testimonials, location, insurance info, and FAQs.
- [Our Team](https://instantorthopediccare.com/team): Meet our board-certified orthopedic surgeons and physician assistants.
- [Book Online](https://pp-wfe-101.advancedmd.com/163338/onlineintake/demographic): Online check-in and appointment scheduling.

## Services

- [Ankle & Foot Care](https://instantorthopediccare.com/services/ankle-and-foot): Treatment for ankle sprains, foot fractures, plantar fasciitis, Achilles tendon injuries, stress fractures, and bunions.
- [Knee & Hip Care](https://instantorthopediccare.com/services/knee-and-hip): Treatment for ACL/MCL tears, meniscus injuries, knee and hip arthritis, patellar disorders, hip bursitis, and joint replacement evaluation.
- [Shoulder Care](https://instantorthopediccare.com/services/shoulder): Treatment for rotator cuff injuries, shoulder dislocations, frozen shoulder, labral (SLAP) tears, AC joint injuries, and shoulder arthritis.
- [Elbow & Wrist Care](https://instantorthopediccare.com/services/elbow-and-wrist): Treatment for tennis elbow, golfer's elbow, carpal tunnel syndrome, wrist and hand fractures, De Quervain's tenosynovitis, and trigger finger.
- [Sports Injury Treatment](https://instantorthopediccare.com/services/sports-injuries): Treatment for ACL and ligament tears, sprains and strains, fractures, concussion evaluation, dislocations, and overuse injuries. Return-to-play clearance for athletes of all ages.
- [Chronic Pain Management](https://instantorthopediccare.com/services/chronic-pain): Treatment for osteoarthritis, inflammatory arthritis, joint injections (corticosteroid and hyaluronic acid), tendonitis, bursitis, back and neck pain, and post-surgical pain management.

## Our Providers

- **Dr. James R. Meadows, MD** — Board-certified, fellowship-trained orthopedic surgeon specializing in sports medicine, ligament reconstruction, and joint preservation. Medical degree from The Ohio State University. Residency at UT Health San Antonio. Fellowship at Tahoe Orthopedics and Sports Medicine.
- **Dr. Christian Sybowsky, MD** — Board-certified orthopedic physician with fellowship training in sports medicine. Medical degree from University of Utah. Residency at University of Washington. Fellowship at University of Iowa Hospitals and Clinics. Specializes in fracture care, sports injuries, and non-operative management.
- **McKay Milne, PA-C** — Orthopedic physician assistant specializing in sports medicine, diagnostic ultrasound, ultrasound-guided procedures, and musculoskeletal injury care.
- **Cole Mitchell, PA-C** — Orthopedic physician assistant specializing in fractures, acute injuries, sports medicine, and injection procedures.

## Key Differentiators

- **Orthopedic specialists, not general practitioners**: Patients are seen by board-certified orthopedic surgeons and fellowship-trained physician assistants—not ER doctors or general urgent care providers.
- **Walk-in convenience**: No appointment, no referral, no waiting weeks to see a specialist. Average wait under 15 minutes.
- **On-site X-ray**: Digital X-ray imaging available during your visit for immediate fracture and injury diagnosis.
- **Fraction of ER cost**: Specialist-level care at up to 75% less than an emergency room visit. With insurance, patients pay their standard specialist copay. Self-pay visits typically $200–$400.
- **Open 7 days a week**: Including weekends for acute injuries and urgent orthopedic needs.
- **Comprehensive treatment on-site**: Casting, splinting, joint injections, bracing, and surgical consultation all available in one visit.

## Frequently Asked Questions

- **Do I need an appointment or referral?** No. Walk-ins are welcome during all business hours. You can also book online.
- **What injuries do you treat?** All orthopedic injuries including fractures, sprains, dislocations, sports injuries, joint pain, and chronic conditions like arthritis.
- **How long will my visit take?** Most patients are seen, diagnosed, and treated within 60 minutes.
- **What if I need surgery?** Our board-certified orthopedic surgeons can evaluate and coordinate surgical care if needed.
- **How much does a visit cost?** Standard specialist copay with insurance. $200–$400 without insurance—up to 75% less than the ER.
- **What insurance do you accept?** BCBS, Aetna, Cigna, UnitedHealthcare, Medicaid, Medicare, workers' compensation, and most major plans.
- **Do you treat children?** Yes, patients of all ages including pediatric fractures, sports injuries, and growth plate concerns.
- **What should I bring?** Photo ID, insurance card (if applicable), and any relevant medical records or prior imaging.
